title: Morgan Stanley DCF Valuation Framework
content: Act as a VP-level investment banker performing a professional Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation. Analyze the company: [ENTER TICKER SYMBOL AND COMPANY NAME]. Build a full DCF model including: 5-year revenue projections with growth assumptions, operating margin estimates based on historical trends, free cash flow calculations for each projected year, Weighted Average Cost of Capital (WACC) estimate, and terminal value using both exit multiple and perpetuity growth methods. Produce a sensitivity table showing fair value under different discount rates. Compare intrinsic value from the DCF model to the current market price and state whether the stock appears undervalued, fairly valued, or overvalued. Clearly list the key assumptions used in the model and highlight factors that could break the valuation. Present the output as an investment banking valuation memo with structured reasoning, tables, and transparent calculations.

title: Bridgewater Portfolio Risk Assessment
content: Act as a senior risk analyst performing an institutional-grade portfolio risk assessment. Analyze the portfolio: [LIST HOLDINGS WITH PERCENTAGES AND TOTAL VALUE]. Evaluate correlation between holdings, sector concentration risk with percentage breakdown, geographic exposure and currency risk, interest rate sensitivity for each position, recession stress test estimating potential drawdown, liquidity risk rating for each holding, and single-stock risk based on position sizing. Identify tail risk scenarios with estimated probabilities. Recommend hedging strategies to mitigate the top three portfolio risks. Provide rebalancing suggestions with specific allocation percentages to improve diversification and resilience. Present the results as a professional risk management report including a portfolio risk heat map summary.

title: JPMorgan Pre-Earnings Analysis Framework
content: Act as a senior equity research analyst preparing a pre-earnings briefing for institutional investors. Analyze the company: [ENTER COMPANY NAME AND EARNINGS DATE]. Provide the last four quarters of earnings results compared to analyst estimates, highlighting beat or miss history. Present consensus revenue and EPS estimates for the upcoming quarter. Identify the key metrics investors are watching for this company and summarize segment-level revenue trends. Summarize management guidance from the last earnings call. Estimate the options market implied move for the upcoming earnings announcement and show historical stock price reactions after the last four earnings releases. Present a bull case scenario with potential upside price impact and a bear case scenario with downside risk. Conclude with a recommendation: buy before earnings, sell before earnings, or wait. Format the response as a concise pre-earnings research brief with a decision summary.

title: BlackRock Institutional Portfolio Builder
content: Act as an institutional portfolio strategist designing a diversified investment portfolio. Build a portfolio based on the investor profile: [AGE, INCOME, SAVINGS, GOALS, RISK TOLERANCE, ACCOUNT TYPE]. Create a strategic asset allocation across equities, bonds, and alternative assets with exact percentage allocations. Recommend specific ETFs or funds with ticker symbols for each asset class. Identify core holdings and satellite positions. Estimate expected annual return range and potential maximum drawdown during severe market conditions. Provide a rebalancing schedule and trigger rules. Include a tax efficiency strategy appropriate for the investor's account type. If the investor contributes regularly, outline a dollar-cost averaging plan. Provide a benchmark index to measure performance. Present the output as a professional investment policy document with clear allocation structure.

title: Citadel Technical Trading Analysis
content: Act as a quantitative trader performing a comprehensive technical analysis. Analyze the stock: [ENTER TICKER SYMBOL AND POSITION IF ANY]. Determine trend direction across daily, weekly, and monthly timeframes. Identify major support and resistance levels with specific price points. Analyze moving averages including the 50-day, 100-day, and 200-day and note any crossover signals. Interpret RSI, MACD, and Bollinger Bands readings in plain language. Examine trading volume trends to evaluate buying versus selling pressure. Identify any recognizable chart patterns such as head and shoulders or cup and handle. Calculate Fibonacci retracement levels to identify potential bounce zones. Provide a recommended entry price, stop-loss level, and profit target with a risk-to-reward ratio. End with a confidence rating: strong buy, buy, neutral, sell, or strong sell. Present the output as a structured technical analysis report with a clear trade plan.

title: Harvard Endowment Dividend Income Strategy
content: Act as an institutional strategist designing a dividend income portfolio. Build a portfolio using the investor information: [TOTAL INVESTMENT AMOUNT, MONTHLY INCOME GOAL, ACCOUNT TYPE, TAX BRACKET]. Select 15–20 dividend stocks with ticker symbols and current dividend yields. Assign a dividend safety score from 1–10 for each company and note consecutive years of dividend growth. Evaluate payout ratios to detect unsustainable dividends. Estimate monthly income generated by the portfolio and project dividend growth over the next five years. Ensure sector diversification to avoid concentration risk. Model dividend reinvestment (DRIP) to illustrate compounding over a 10-year period. Summarize tax considerations for dividend income based on the account type. Rank the stocks from safest to most aggressive and present the output as a dividend portfolio blueprint with an income projection table.

title: Bain Competitive Industry Stock Analysis
content: Act as a strategy consultant conducting a competitive industry analysis for investors. Analyze the sector: [ENTER INDUSTRY OR SECTOR NAME]. Identify the top 5–7 companies in the sector and compare market capitalization, revenue, and profit margins in a structured table. Evaluate each company's competitive moat including brand strength, cost advantages, network effects, and switching costs. Analyze market share trends over the last three years. Assess management quality based on capital allocation history. Compare innovation pipelines and R&D spending. Identify the biggest threats facing the sector such as regulatory pressure, disruption, or macroeconomic factors. Perform SWOT analysis for the top two companies. Conclude with the single most attractive stock in the sector and explain the rationale along with potential catalysts over the next 12 months.

title: Renaissance Quant Pattern Detection
content: Act as a quantitative researcher identifying statistical patterns in a stock. Analyze: [ENTER TICKER SYMBOL AND TIME PERIOD]. Investigate seasonal patterns such as historically strong and weak months. Examine day-of-week performance anomalies. Study correlations with macro events such as Federal Reserve meetings or inflation reports. Review insider buying and selling trends from filings. Analyze institutional ownership changes to determine whether major funds are accumulating or distributing shares. Evaluate short interest and the potential for a short squeeze. Identify unusual options activity. Study historical price behavior around earnings announcements including pre-earnings runs or post-earnings gaps. Assess sector rotation signals affecting the stock. Conclude with a statistical edge summary describing any quantifiable advantage or repeatable pattern discovered.

title: McKinsey Macro Impact Portfolio Analysis
content: Act as a macroeconomic strategist evaluating how global economic conditions affect an investment portfolio. Analyze the portfolio: [LIST PORTFOLIO AND ECONOMIC CONCERNS]. Assess the current interest rate environment and its impact on growth versus value stocks. Evaluate inflation trends and which sectors benefit or suffer. Review GDP growth forecasts and implications for corporate earnings. Analyze the strength of the US dollar and its impact on international versus domestic holdings. Examine employment trends and consumer spending indicators. Evaluate the Federal Reserve policy outlook for the next 6–12 months. Identify major global risks including geopolitical tensions, supply chain disruptions, or trade conflicts. Recommend sector rotation strategies and specific portfolio adjustments. Provide a timeline of when these macro forces are likely to impact markets.
